rulings.Calendar Matter: Hearing Re Contempt
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, MAY 15, 2007, LINE 4. HEARING RE CONTEMPT: PLAINTIFF ORDERED TO PAY THE SLAPP FEE AWARD OF $10,425.00, FEES AND COSTS OF $36.30, OR POST A BOND WITHIN SEVEN DAYS FOR ONE AND A HALF TIMES THAT AMOUNT PURSUANT TO DOWLING V ZIMMERMAN (2001) 85 CAL APP 4TH 1400, 1429-1444. PLAINTIFF ORDERED TO PAY $5,000.00 IN ATTORNEYS' FEES AND COSTS FOR BRINGING THIS MOTION. (302/PJM/KD) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

rulings.Case Title: PHILIP MILLENBAH VS. GREG BURKE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgement On The Pleadings;Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, MAY 15, 2007, LINE 6. DEFENDANT GREG BURKE'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings IS DENIED AS TO THE FIRST AND SECOND CAUSES OF ACTION. SUSTAINED AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE FACTS TO SUPPORT THIS CLAIM THAT HAVE ARISEN AFTER THE DISMISSAL OF THE SAN DIEGO ACTION. THE MOTION RELATES ONLY TO BURKE IN HIS INDIVIDUAL CAPACITY. PLAINTIFF ENTITLED TO SANCTIONS OF ONE-THIRD OF REASONABLE ATTORNEY FEES AND COSTS FOR THE MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. THE MOTION AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION WAS FRIVILOUS. =(302/PJM/AP)

rulings.Case Title: JULIANA AGARDI VS. NCPHS SEQUOIAS SAN FRANCISCO
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, MAY 15, 2007, LINE 8. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND WITHIN TWENTY DAYS. THE COMPLAINT IS UNCERTAIN. PLAINTIFF MUST FILE A FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT THAT IS NOT ALL IN CAPITAL LETTERS, THAT STATES SEPARATELY EACH CAUSE OF ACTION AND IDENTIFIES THE PARTY OR PARTIES TO WHOM THE CAUSE OF ACTION IS DIRECTED. =(302/PJM/AP)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: STEPHEN WELKON
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order That All Intra-Family Claims Be Arbitrated Together In One Arbitration Proceeding
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, MAY 15, 2007, LINE 15. PETITIONER STEPHEN WELKOM, DANETTE WELKOM, STEPHEN WELCOM AND DANETTE WELCOM'S Motion For Order That All Intra-Family Claims Be Arbitrated Together In One Arbitration Proceeding: COURT DENIES THE REQUEST TO TRANSFER THE MATTER TO JUDGE QUIDACHAY. GRANT REQUEST TO CONSOLIDATE INTRA-FAMILY CLAIMS. THIS A DISTINCT ISSUE FROM THE ISSUE ADDRESSED BY JUDGE QUIDACHAY. DENY REQUEST RE DISCOVERY FOR THAT IS TO BE ADDRESSED IN THE ARBITRATION. =(302/PJM/KD)

rulings.Case Title: BLOOMBERG NEWS VS. CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF INSURANCE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Mandate
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, MAY 15, 2007, LINE 16. PETITIONER BLOOMBERG NEWS' Petition For Writ Of Mandate: HEARING REQUIRED TO ADDRESS WHETHER "LICENSING" INVESTIGATIONS ARE ABSOLUTELY PRIVILEGED UNDER GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION 6254(F) AND FOR THE STATE TO ADDRESS HOW, UNDER ANY BALANCING TEST, NON-DISCLOSURE IS MANDATED GIVEN THE FACT THAT THE CLAIMS RELATE TO 1998 - 1999 EVENTS AND THE MATTERS HAVE BEEN RESOLVED. =(302/PJM/KD)

rulings.Case Title: SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY VS. JACQUELINA LAURENT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion, & Motion In Support To Set Aside Judgment
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, MAY 15, 2007, LINE 17. DEFENDANT JACQUELINA LAURENT'S Motion In Support To Set Aside Judgment IS DENIED. DEFENDANT FAILED TO PROVIDE ANY EXPLANATION WHY SHE DID NOT OPPOSE PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO REINSTATE. DEFENDANT ALSO HAS NOT MET THE REQUIREMENTS OF C.C.P. SECTION 473. =(302/PJM/AP)

rulings.Case Title: SUKDEV PATEL VS. ROBERT EASTVELD

rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, MAY 15, 2007, LINE 19. DEFENDANT ROBERT EASTVELD'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT: HEARING REQUIRED TO ADDRESS: (1) MS. GOLD'S ROLE AND APPLICATION OF 473 (B). (2) THE AMOUNT OF RENT NOW DUE UNDER THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT; AND (3) WHETHER PLAINTIFF GAVE TWENTY-FOUR HOURS NOTICE TO DEFENDANT PRIOR TO SEEKING EX PARTE JUDGMENT. =(302/PJM/AP)
